author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

title: John 1.9

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That was the true Light, which lighteth every man that cometh into the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The reason, O children greatly beloved, why we entertain you portion by portion with the thoughts taken from the Scriptures, and do not at once pour all forth to you, is, that the retaining what is successively set before you may be easy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For even in building, one who before the first stones are settled lays on others, constructs a rotten wall altogether, and easily thrown down: while one who waits that the mortar may first get hard, and so adds what remains little by little, finishes the whole house firmly, and makes it strong, not one to last for a short time, or easily to fall to pieces.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These builders we imitate, and in like manner build up your souls.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For we fear lest, while the first foundation is but newly laid, the addition of the succeeding speculations may do harm to the former, through the insufficiency of the intellect to contain them all at once.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What now is it that has been read to us today?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That was the true Light, which lighteth every man that cometh into the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since above in speaking of John he said, that he came “to bear witness of that Light”; and that he was sent in these our days; lest any one at hearing this should, on account of the recent coming of the witness, conceive some like suspicion concerning Him, who is witnessed of, he has carried up the imagination, and transported it to that existence which is before all beginning, which has neither end nor commencement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And how is it possible,” says one, “that being a Son, He should possess this (nature)?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We are speaking of God, and do you ask how?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And do you not fear nor shudder?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet should any one ask you, “How should our souls and bodies have endless life in the world to come?” you will laugh at the question, on the ground that it does not belong to the intellect of man to search into such questions, but that he ought only to believe, and not to be over-curious on the subject mentioned, since he has a sufficient proof of the saying, in the power of Him who spake it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if we say, that He, who created our souls and bodies, and who incomparably excels all created things, is without beginning, will you require us to say “How?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who could assert this to be the act of a well-ordered soul, or of sound reason? you have heard that “That was the true Light”: why are you vainly and rashly striving to overshoot by force of reasoning this Life which is unlimited?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: You cannot do it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why seek what may not be sought?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why be curious about what is incomprehensible?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why search what is unsearchable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Gaze upon the very source of the sunbeams.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: You cannot; yet you are neither vexed nor impatient at your weakness; how then have you become so daring and headlong in greater matters?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The son of thunder, John who sounds the spiritual trumpet, when he had heard from the Spirit the was, enquired no farther.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And are you, who share not in his grace, but speak from your own wretched reasonings, ambitious to exceed the measure of his knowledge?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then for this very reason you will never be able even to reach to the measure of his knowledge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is the craft of the devil: he leads away those who obey him from the limits assigned by God, as though to things much greater: but when, having enticed us by these hopes, he has cast us out of the grace of God, he not only gives nothing more, (how can he, devil as he is?) but does not even allow us to return again to our former situation, where we dwelt safely and surely, but leads us about in all directions wandering and not having any standing ground.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So he caused the first created man to be banished from the abode of Paradise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having puffed him up with the expectation of greater knowledge and honor, he expelled him from what he already possessed in security.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he not only did not become like a god as (the devil) promised him, but even fell beneath the dominion of death; having not only gained no further advantage by eating of the tree, but having lost no small portion of the knowledge which he possessed, through hope of greater knowledge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the sense of shame, and the desire to hide himself because of his nakedness, then came upon him, who before the cheat was superior to all such shame; and this very seeing himself to be naked, and the need for the future of the covering of garments, and many other infirmities, became thenceforth natural to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That this be not our case, let us obey God, continue in His commandments, and not be busy about anything beyond them, that we may not be cast out from the good things already given us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus they have fared (of whom we speak).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For seeking to find a beginning of the Life which has no beginning, they lost what they might have retained.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They found not what they sought, (this is impossible,) and they fell away from the true faith concerning the Only-Begotten.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not then remove the eternal bounds which our fathers set, but let us ever yield to the laws of the Spirit; and when we hear that “That was the true Light,” let us seek to discover nothing more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is not possible to pass beyond this saying.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had His generation been like that of a man, needs must there have been an interval between the begetter and the begotten; but since it is in a manner ineffable and becoming God, give up the “before” and the “after,” for these are the names of points in time, but the Son is the Creator even of all ages.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then,” says one, “He is not Father, but brother.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What need, pray?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we had asserted that the Father and the Son were from a different root, you might have then spoken this well.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But, if we flee this impiety, and say the Father, besides being without beginning, is Unbegotten also, while the Son, though without beginning, is Begotten of the Father, what kind of need that as a consequence of this idea, that unholy assertion should be introduced?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: None at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He is an Effulgence: but an effulgence is included in the idea of the nature whose effulgence it is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this reason Paul has called Him so, that you may imagine no interval between the Father and the Son. ( Heb. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This expression therefore is declaratory of the point; but the following part of the proof quoted, corrects an erroneous opinion which might beset simple men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, says the Apostle, do not, because you have heard that he is an Effulgence, suppose that He is deprived of His proper person; this is impious, and belongs to the madness of the Sabellians, and of Marcellus’ followers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We say not so, but that He is also in His proper Person.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And for this reason, after having called Him “Effulgence,” Paul has added that He is “the express image of His Person” ( Heb. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3.), in order to make evident His proper Personality, and that He belongs to the same Essence of which He is also the express image.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, as I before said, it is not sufficient by a single expression to set before men the doctrines concerning God, but it is desirable that we bring many together, and choose from each what is suitable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So shall we be able to attain to a worthy telling of His glory, worthy, I mean, as regards our power; for if any should deem himself able to speak words suitable to His essential worthiness, and be ambitious to do so, saying, that he knows God as God knows Himself, he it is who is most ignorant of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Knowing therefore this, let us continue steadfastly to hold what “they have delivered unto us, which from the beginning were eye-witnesses, and ministers of the word.” ( Luke i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And let us not be curious beyond: for two evils will attend those who are sick of this disease, (curiosity,) the wearying themselves in vain by seeking what it is impossible to find, and the provoking God by their endeavors to overturn the bounds set by Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now what anger this excites, it needs not that you who know should learn from us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Abstaining therefore from their madness, let us tremble at His words, that He may continually build us up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “upon whom shall I look” ( Isa. lxvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 2 , LXX.), saith He, “but upon the lowly, and quiet, and who feareth my words?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then leave this pernicious curiosity, and bruise our hearts, let us mourn for our sins as Christ commanded, let us be pricked at heart for our transgressions, let us reckon up exactly all the wicked deeds, which in time past we have dared, and let us earnestly strive to wipe them off in all kinds of ways.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now to this end God hath opened to us many ways.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “Tell thou first,” saith He, “thy sins, that thou mayest be justified” ( Isa. xliii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 26); and again, “I said, I have declared mine iniquity unto Thee, and Thou hast taken away the unrighteousness of my heart” ( Ps. xxxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 , LXX.); since a continual accusation and remembrance of sins contributes not a little to lessen their magnitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But there is another more prevailing way than this; to bear malice against none of those who have offended against us, to forgive their trespasses to all those who have trespassed against us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Will you learn a third?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear Daniel, saying, “Redeem thy sins by almsdeeds, and thine iniquities by showing mercy to the poor.” ( Dan. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And there is another besides this; constancy in prayer, and persevering attendance on the intercessions made with God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In like manner fasting brings to us some, and that not small comfort and release from sins committed, provided it be attended with kindness to others, and quenches the vehemence of the wrath of God. ( 1 Tim. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “water will quench a blazing fire, and by almsdeeds sins are purged away.” ( Ecclus. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then travel along all these ways; for if we give ourselves wholly to these employments, if on them we spend our time, not only shall we wash off our bygone transgressions, but shall gain very great profit for the future.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For we shall not allow the devil to assault us with leisure either for slothful living, or for pernicious curiosity, since by these among other means, and in consequence of these, he leads us to foolish questions and hurtful disputations, from seeing us at leisure, and idle, and taking no forethought for excellency of living.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But let us block up this approach against him, let us watch, let us be sober, that having in this short time toiled a little, we may obtain eternal goods in endless ages, by the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ; by whom and with whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost, be glory for ever and ever.
